title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. raichei
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. raichei, commonly known as Raiche's lepidium, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the mustard family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The plant is rich in compounds with potential health benefits, including antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Raiche's lepidium include infusions and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
